ocr.text: In the fiscal year 2023, SoftBank Corp reported significant metrics in its IoT connection growth, reflecting its strategic
positioning in the telecommunications sector within the Asia-Pacific region. The total connections reached 2.5 million, while
the average revenue per user (ARPU) stood at HKD150, demonstrating a robust monetary value per connection. The churn rate,
an essential indicator of customer retention, was recorded at 5.2%, suggesting a relatively stable customer base as the company
navigates a competitive market landscape. Additionally, the comparative index of the IoT connection metrics indicated a
noteworthy growth rate of 15.3 million connections, which underscores the increasing adoption of IoT solutions.
The corresponding ARPU for this segment was HKD125, reflecting a slight decrease compared to the smaller connection
segment, which may imply different pricing strategies or customer profiles. Furthermore, the churn rate for this larger group
was 6.1%, which is higher than that of the 2.5 million connections, signaling potential challenges in maintaining customer
loyalty within a rapidly evolving technological environment. Overall, these figures encapsulate SoftBank Corp's performance in
the IoT landscape, showcasing both the opportunities and challenges faced as it continues to expand its market share and
enhance its service offerings amidst a backdrop of growing demand for IoT connectivity in the region.
